title: IDDD Bibliographic Inventory

fields.vision: IDDD Bibliographic Inventory is a source packet for this package is the final handoff of a source-grounded field/discipline inventory extracted from the IDDD manuscript (Individual in the Democratic Dialectic, IDDD.docx, SHA-256 b0bd6d3f6db4be8cbc230c9371b08f10b56a1ea1221ad8524802cc73810f77e0, 40,838 paragraphs, 38,345 nonempty blocks, 8 tables, 3,596,306 characters, ~518,804 words).
fields.mechanism: , state the backend/frontend or packet components, and decide whether the handoff is ready for engineering, public explanation, or archival retention
fields.source_utilization: Political theory, democracy, individuality, and civic formation. Use source library political theory as a central source reservoir for the Book Trilogy and democratic-formation pages. Vision: Give Book II, democracy, and civic-formation pages a political-theory spine rather than generic civics language. Mechanism: Extract liberty, equality, tyranny, statesmanship, party government, normativity, democracy, and Machiavellian institutional mechanisms. Plan: Close-read Book Trilogy and political-theory pages before importing formulations. Status: source library identified as candidate scholarly support; direct quotation requires exact passage review. Remaining gap: Need exact passages before public quotations or stronger scholarly claims. Source pool: 76 deduplicated local title candidates; 80 catalogue support rows. Examples: On Liberalism; Democracy and Its Fragility. Discovery, creativity, systems thinking, and project generation. Use source library creativity/innovation/network sources to professionalize the project-development account. Vision: Explain Nexus and Personal OS as project-development and discovery infrastructure, not miscellaneous personal tooling. Mechanism: Extract creativity, network, learning, imagination, leadership, and project-generation mechanisms. Plan: Rewrite Nexus and planning pages around upstream/downstream project movement. Status: source library identified as candidate scholarly support; direct quotation requires exact passage review. Remaining gap: Need exact project-method and creativity passages. Source pool: 151 deduplicated local title candidates; 154 catalogue support rows. Examples: Creativity Research; The Heritage, Creativity and Innovation Nexus. source library scholarly-source routing with author correction. Raw filenames often append Nik_Murano and are not treated as authorship evidence. Catalogue rows are...
fields.plan: How this briefing says the vision should be accomplished, translated into a public project route. Open 01_README.md for orientation — verify gate in 00_STATUS_DONE.txt — read the full inventory via 02_grouped_inventory.md (human-readable) or exports/03_master_inventory.csv/.xlsx/.json (machine-readable). Use 07_scope_counts.csv to understand balance (283 core / 120 applied / 45 project-specific / 9 boundary). Use 09_72_chapter_domain_map.csv when you need to trace a chapter to its domains. Consult qa/08_rejected_unverified_candidates.csv before reintroducing any dropped domain — it shows what was considered and rejected. Verify package integrity with qa/11_integrity_manifest.json. Wire or preserve the implementation files: None — this is an analysis handoff, not a code package. The exports are CSV/JSON/XLSX/TXT for downstream consumption (e.g., building bibliographies, syllabi, or committee reviews).
fields.remaining_gaps: Retain the packet as a completed authority layer, then decide whether it should remain a reference file or become a live room, method page, or public proof object.
fields.detailed_source_vision: This package is the final handoff of a source-grounded field/discipline inventory extracted from the IDDD manuscript (Individual in the Democratic Dialectic, IDDD.docx, SHA-256 b0bd6d3f6db4be8cbc230c9371b08f10b56a1ea1221ad8524802cc73810f77e0, 40,838 paragraphs, 38,345 nonempty blocks, 8 tables, 3,596,306 characters, ~518,804 words). Its deliverable is a verified catalog of every academic field, subfield, applied specialty, and project-specific analytic frame the manuscript draws on — each entry provably tethered to the source. The governing rule is a hard no-phantom-entries gate: every kept inventory entry has (a) an evidence term or phrase, (b) a paragraph/table pointer into the extracted DOCX, and (c) a short evidence excerpt. Candidates that could not be directly located using the final verification terms are not kept — they are preserved separately in qa/08_rejected_unverified_candidates.csv (14 rejections) rather than silently dropped. This...

maintenance_rule: Update this blueprint before strengthening public claims on the website. Do not use word count, module count, or status percentage as a substitute for concrete project substance.
claim_ceiling_rule: Public claims may rise only when the built components, source use, status, and remaining gaps are updated here and reflected in the rendered page.

public_standard_check.Vision: This document supports the public account of Blueprint by preserving the project purpose, reader-facing claim, and professional-portfolio role.
public_standard_check.Mechanism: It connects source material, project components, implementation state, and next actions so the work can be inspected rather than merely asserted.
public_standard_check.What is built: The supporting evidence is the named page, blueprint, packet, project file, source record, or implementation page described in the document.
public_standard_check.Source use: Sources are used as method and source background, domain grounding, or project history; direct quotation and stronger factual claims require passage-level review.
public_standard_check.Plan: Bind claims to exact source anchors, visible implementation evidence, or a narrower public claim.
public_standard_check.Status: Current support record for the professional package unless explicitly marked archival.
public_standard_check.Remaining Gaps: Passage-level source check, implementation verification, citation cleanup, and proportional public wording remain open where evidence is incomplete.
